description: A rich layered pastry made with thin sheets of phyllo, chopped nuts, butter or ghee, and a sweet syrup or honey-based glaze. It is typically cut into diamonds or squares before or after baking.
core_ingredients: Chopped nuts such as pistachios, walnuts, or almonds
preparation_steps: Layer sheets of phyllo with melted butter or ghee.
Spread nut filling between or across selected layers.
Cut the unbaked pastry into portions.
Pour cooled or warm syrup over the baked pastry and let it absorb before serving.

health_advice.benefits: Nuts contribute some unsaturated fat and minerals.
health_advice.risks: Usually very energy-dense because it combines pastry, butter, nuts, and syrup.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Serve small portions; reducing syrup or using more nuts relative to syrup can make it less sweet.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional versions are vegetarian. Vegan versions use dairy-free fat and avoid honey if strict vegan standards are required.

common_variations: Regional versions using different nuts, spices, or shapes

best_served_with: Unsweetened yogurt in some regional pairings
